message:
So my partner loves to leave the stovetop unattended while cooking. *Loves* it. I've mentioned the fire thing maybe... a thousand times? 😅 Last week he stepped away mid-sear, and I caught myself doing that annoying-but-justified hovering thing. He rolled his eyes at me *hard*. Then the pan flared up just enough to set off the smoke alarm—nothing serious, just a reminder that yeah, kitchens need attention. The *look* on his face. I didn't even say "I told you so." I didn't have to. 🙃

But honestly, that smoke alarm doing its job gave me a little peace of mind. It made me realize how easy it is to brush off the small stuff—staying in the kitchen while cooking, testing alarms, having an escape plan—until something actually happens. The little things really do matter.

Has anyone else had that "told you so" moment that actually felt good because it meant everyone was okay?
